answers.text: Make your turn from the farthest lane to the direction you want to go.

answers.explanation: Turns must be executed from the lane closest to your intended direction and into the lane closest to the corresponding direction on the new road.

answers.text: Turn from any lane that is available.
Always turn from the leftmost lane.

explanation: Turns must be executed from the lane closest to your intended direction and into the lane closest to the corresponding direction on the new road.

question: What does it signify if the center of the road has one solid yellow line and one broken yellow line next to your lane?

answers.text: Passing is prohibited in this zone under any conditions.
You may overtake other vehicles on the left when it is safe, as long as there is no incoming traffic.
answers.explanation: A broken yellow line alongside your lane allows for passing if it is safe and there is no oncoming traffic.

answers.text: The area is designated for right turns only.

explanation: A broken yellow line alongside your lane allows for passing if it is safe and there is no oncoming traffic.

question: Is it ever appropriate to drive around a railway crossing barrier that is coming down?

answers.text: Never, it is too dangerous.
answers.explanation: This action is illegal and risky.

answers.text: When there is malfunction in the gate’s mechanism.
If there is enough time before the gate closes fully.
If there is a clear shoulder beside the gate.

explanation: This action is illegal and risky.

question: When operating a large truck or bus, how should you approach driving on the highway?

answers.text: Must be constantly mindful of the safety of other road users.
answers.explanation: Driving a heavy truck or bus requires being constantly aware of the vehicle's potential for causing severe damage and injury. The safety of all road users, including passengers, other drivers, and pedestrians, must be a priority.

answers.text: Can utilize your vehicle's size to assert space on the road.
Should assume smaller vehicles will yield to you.
Only need to focus on the safety of your vehicle and cargo.

explanation: Driving a heavy truck or bus requires being constantly aware of the vehicle's potential for causing severe damage and injury. The safety of all road users, including passengers, other drivers, and pedestrians, must be a priority.

question: When approaching a vehicle at night that is using high beams:

answers.text: Briefly flick your high beams to alert the other driver.
answers.explanation: If an oncoming vehicle has high beams on at night, briefly flick your high beams to signal the driver. If the lights remain undimmed, look toward the right edge of the road to avoid glare. Do not use your high beams to escalate the situation.

answers.text: Keep using your high beams.
Stare directly at the oncoming lights.

explanation: If an oncoming vehicle has high beams on at night, briefly flick your high beams to signal the driver. If the lights remain undimmed, look toward the right edge of the road to avoid glare. Do not use your high beams to escalate the situation.

question: Under which circumstance must you stop for a school bus?

answers.text: That is stopped on the opposite side of a divided highway.
When it is approaching but without flashing lights.
On a two-lane road with its red lights flashing.
answers.explanation: You must stop on a two-lane road when a school bus has its red lights flashing, indicating that children are getting on or off.

answers.text: When the school bus pulls over to let traffic pass.

explanation: You must stop on a two-lane road when a school bus has its red lights flashing, indicating that children are getting on or off.

question: If you are pulled over by the police, what documents must you provide?

answers.text: Just their proof of registration and insurance.
Only their driver's license.
Driver's license, registration, and proof of insurance.
answers.explanation: When pulled over, a motorist must show their driver's license, vehicle registration, and proof of insurance. These documents are required whenever the vehicle is being driven.

explanation: When pulled over, a motorist must show their driver's license, vehicle registration, and proof of insurance. These documents are required whenever the vehicle is being driven.

question: How should you regard all railroad crossings?

answers.text: Safe areas to park a vehicle.
Always hazardous.
answers.explanation: Railroad crossings are inherently dangerous and require drivers to stop, look, and listen for oncoming trains before crossing.

answers.text: Places where stopping is required.
Likely free of approaching trains, so generally safe to cross.

explanation: Railroad crossings are inherently dangerous and require drivers to stop, look, and listen for oncoming trains before crossing.

question: When operating a vehicle in areas with light rail systems, you should always:

answers.text: Look out for trains on all tracks at any moment.
Follow all traffic signs and signals carefully.
Exercise caution when making turns across rail tracks.
All of the above.
answers.explanation: When you are near light rail vehicles, you must expect trains on any track at any time, obey all signs and signals, and always check both directions before crossing.

explanation: When you are near light rail vehicles, you must expect trains on any track at any time, obey all signs and signals, and always check both directions before crossing.

question: What kind of information do blue traffic signs provide?

answers.text: Guidelines for driving safely.
Directions to various locations.
Available services for motorists.
answers.explanation: Blue signs denote available services for drivers, such as rest stops, gas stations, and medical facilities.

explanation: Blue signs denote available services for drivers, such as rest stops, gas stations, and medical facilities.

question: Driving while extremely tired has similar effects to what?

answers.text: The impact of alcohol consumption.
answers.explanation: Being extremely fatigued can impair your driving in a way similar to alcohol. For example, staying awake for 18 hours can affect your driving like a 0.05% blood alcohol concentration. Staying awake for 24 hours impacts it akin to a 0.10% blood alcohol concentration.

answers.text: The impact of taking amphetamines.
The impact of experiencing road rage.
The impact of having loud music in the car.

explanation: Being extremely fatigued can impair your driving in a way similar to alcohol. For example, staying awake for 18 hours can affect your driving like a 0.05% blood alcohol concentration. Staying awake for 24 hours impacts it akin to a 0.10% blood alcohol concentration.

question: When approaching an intersection with an obstructed view of oncoming traffic, what is the proper action to take?

answers.text: Reduce speed and scan the area.
Proceed without adjusting speed, while checking both ways.
Come to a full stop, then carefully move forward to get a clear view.
answers.explanation: When visibility is obstructed at an intersection, drivers should stop completely and cautiously advance until they can clearly see traffic from all directions.

explanation: When visibility is obstructed at an intersection, drivers should stop completely and cautiously advance until they can clearly see traffic from all directions.

question: What is the maximum legal blood alcohol concentration (BAC) for drivers aged 21 and older?

answers.text: 0.04 percent.
0.06 percent.
0.08 percent.
answers.explanation: For individuals aged 21 or older, it is unlawful to operate a vehicle with a blood alcohol level of 0.08 percent or more. DUI laws are enforced to ensure road safety.

answers.text: 0.1 percent.

explanation: For individuals aged 21 or older, it is unlawful to operate a vehicle with a blood alcohol level of 0.08 percent or more. DUI laws are enforced to ensure road safety.

question: Which of these statements about following distance are correct?

answers.text: Experienced drivers might be able to follow at a two-second interval in normal conditions.
Newer drivers should aim for a minimum three-second gap in regular situations.
Driving over 40 mph requires a four- to five-second following distance for safety.
All of the above.
answers.explanation: In good conditions and speeds under 40 mph, experienced drivers can aim for a two-second gap, while new drivers should keep a three-second gap. For speeds above 40 mph, a four- to five-second gap is recommended for all drivers. Adjust these distances to be longer under poor conditions.

explanation: In good conditions and speeds under 40 mph, experienced drivers can aim for a two-second gap, while new drivers should keep a three-second gap. For speeds above 40 mph, a four- to five-second gap is recommended for all drivers. Adjust these distances to be longer under poor conditions.

question: What should drivers do when they see a red arrow on a traffic light?

answers.text: Drivers should slowly proceed in the direction of the arrow.
Drivers should stop and wait until it is safe to go.
Drivers must stop and wait until the arrow turns green or a green light appears.
answers.explanation: A red arrow indicates that drivers must stop and wait until a green arrow or green light is displayed before proceeding in the indicated direction.

answers.text: Drivers may turn in the direction of the red arrow.

explanation: A red arrow indicates that drivers must stop and wait until a green arrow or green light is displayed before proceeding in the indicated direction.

question: While driving in sleet or snow, what measures should you take?

answers.text: Ensure that all windows and mirrors are free of ice and snow.
Increase the gap between you and the car in front of you.
Exercise extreme caution around other vehicles.
All of the above.
answers.explanation: In snowy conditions, it's crucial to keep your windows and mirrors clear, increase following distance, and drive cautiously due to reduced visibility and slippery roads.

explanation: In snowy conditions, it's crucial to keep your windows and mirrors clear, increase following distance, and drive cautiously due to reduced visibility and slippery roads.

question: How should you respond when a school bus is stopped with flashing red lights?

answers.text: You may proceed only when children are not visible.
You must stop until the red lights stop flashing.
answers.explanation: When a school bus has stopped and its red lights are flashing, you must stop your vehicle at least 20 feet away and remain stopped until the lights stop flashing or you are signaled to proceed. This rule applies even on divided highways in New York State.

answers.text: You can pass only if you approach from the opposite direction.
You may pass if you see the front of the bus.

explanation: When a school bus has stopped and its red lights are flashing, you must stop your vehicle at least 20 feet away and remain stopped until the lights stop flashing or you are signaled to proceed. This rule applies even on divided highways in New York State.

question: Which of the following statements is accurate regarding right turns?

answers.text: You must swing wide to the left before making a right turn to clear the curb.
Turning right on red is generally permitted after stopping, unless a sign indicates otherwise.
answers.explanation: After stopping and yielding to pedestrians and other traffic, you can turn right at a red light unless a sign prohibits it. Ensure you stay close to the right edge or curb throughout the turn.

answers.text: Upon completing a right turn onto a multi-lane road, you must end up in the leftmost lane.

explanation: After stopping and yielding to pedestrians and other traffic, you can turn right at a red light unless a sign prohibits it. Ensure you stay close to the right edge or curb throughout the turn.

question: What behaviors are risky when navigating through a construction zone?

answers.text: Driving too closely to the car ahead
Not paying attention to construction crews and machinery
Both of these actions
answers.explanation: In construction areas, it’s important to maintain a safe distance to the car ahead and stay vigilant for workers and equipment.

explanation: In construction areas, it’s important to maintain a safe distance to the car ahead and stay vigilant for workers and equipment.

question: What happens when driving at high speeds on a wet road?

answers.text: Increasing your speed reduces your tires' ability to channel water out of the way.
answers.explanation: Driving faster on wet roads can cause your tires to lose their effectiveness in diverting water, increasing the risk of hydroplaning.

answers.text: High-quality tires are unaffected by wet roadways.
It is less risky to drive through deeper water than shallow water.
Slowing down makes the wet road more dangerous.

explanation: Driving faster on wet roads can cause your tires to lose their effectiveness in diverting water, increasing the risk of hydroplaning.

question: While waiting to make a left turn, what should you do?

answers.text: Turn your wheels to the left while signaling.
Keep your wheels straight and signal your intention.
answers.explanation: Ensures you are ready to turn safely when the path is clear.

answers.text: Honk your horn to alert other drivers.
Drive to the right of the vehicle blocking your way.

explanation: Ensures you are ready to turn safely when the path is clear.

question: What should you do when you see a pedestrian with a white cane or a guide dog?

answers.text: Always yield to them.
answers.explanation: Pedestrians using a white cane or guide dog must be given the right-of-way, as they are visually impaired.

answers.text: Accelerate to pass them quickly.
Sound your horn to warn them.

explanation: Pedestrians using a white cane or guide dog must be given the right-of-way, as they are visually impaired.

question: When driving a friend's car that you have never driven before, what should you do?

answers.text: Assume the car operates the same as every other vehicle you have driven.
Avoid mentioning to your friend that you're not used to their car.
Ignore any visible issues to avoid awkwardness with your friend.
Spend some time getting acquainted with the car's controls and checking for any obvious problems.
answers.explanation: Understanding the unique controls and defects of an unfamiliar vehicle is critical for safe operation.

explanation: Understanding the unique controls and defects of an unfamiliar vehicle is critical for safe operation.

question: On a two-lane road with traffic moving in opposite directions, how should you drive?

answers.text: You can travel in either lane at any time.
Pick the lane with fewer cars.
Stick to the right lane unless passing another vehicle legally.
answers.explanation: On a two-way road, you must use the right lane unless you're overtaking another vehicle in a legal manner.

answers.text: You may use the left lane if you're preparing to make a left turn.

explanation: On a two-way road, you must use the right lane unless you're overtaking another vehicle in a legal manner.

question: When encountering a solid red HAWK signal, what action is required of drivers?

answers.text: Drivers should come to a full stop.
answers.explanation: HAWK beacons are used to assist pedestrian crossings. A solid red signal means drivers must come to a full stop.

answers.text: Drivers must slow down and proceed with caution.
Drivers can continue driving normally.
The crosswalk is not in use.

explanation: HAWK beacons are used to assist pedestrian crossings. A solid red signal means drivers must come to a full stop.

question: How many seconds of following distance should you maintain in good driving conditions?

answers.text: Three to four seconds.
answers.explanation: In normal driving conditions, it is recommended to keep a following distance of three to four seconds. This distance should be increased if driving conditions are poor, such as during rain or heavy traffic.

answers.text: Two seconds.
Five to seven seconds.
Ten seconds.

explanation: In normal driving conditions, it is recommended to keep a following distance of three to four seconds. This distance should be increased if driving conditions are poor, such as during rain or heavy traffic.

question: Which of the following is accurate regarding driving at higher speeds?

answers.text: Higher speeds increase the likelihood of a deadly accident.
You should maintain a greater following distance at higher speeds.
Both statements are True.
answers.explanation: When you drive faster, you are at greater risk of a fatal crash. Additionally, increased speed necessitates a longer following distance to ensure you have enough time to stop safely.

explanation: When you drive faster, you are at greater risk of a fatal crash. Additionally, increased speed necessitates a longer following distance to ensure you have enough time to stop safely.

question: What is a consequence of driving at higher speeds?

answers.text: Worsens your peripheral vision.
Limits your overall view.
answers.explanation: Increasing speed reduces the range of your visual field, making it harder to notice surroundings.

answers.text: Enhances your ability to spot hazards.
Does not affect your field of view.

explanation: Increasing speed reduces the range of your visual field, making it harder to notice surroundings.

question: What does a solid yellow line next to a dashed yellow line signify?

answers.text: Only vehicles next to the dashed line may overtake.
Vehicles on both sides are allowed to pass.
Vehicles adjacent to the continuous line may overtake.

question: In which situations should you avoid passing another vehicle?

answers.text: When your view is obstructed.
In areas with restricted passing zones.
If it interferes with oncoming vehicles.
All of the above.
answers.explanation: Drivers must avoid passing in situations where visibility is limited, in no-passing zones, or when it endangers themselves and others.

explanation: Drivers must avoid passing in situations where visibility is limited, in no-passing zones, or when it endangers themselves and others.

question: After having a heated argument and feeling angry, what should you do before driving?

answers.text: Turn on some loud music to distract yourself from your feelings.
Wait until you calm down before getting behind the wheel.
answers.explanation: Emotions can impair your driving abilities. If you're feeling upset, it's best to give yourself time to relax and regain composure before driving.

answers.text: Drive fast to help release your pent-up emotions.

explanation: Emotions can impair your driving abilities. If you're feeling upset, it's best to give yourself time to relax and regain composure before driving.

question: When driving on a one-way street, under which condition can you make a left turn onto another street?

answers.text: The opposing traffic does not have a stop sign.
There is traffic moving against you.
The traffic on the other street is also one-way going left.
answers.explanation: You can turn left onto another one-way street only if the traffic on that street also flows to the left.

explanation: You can turn left onto another one-way street only if the traffic on that street also flows to the left.

question: What should you do when an emergency vehicle with sirens is approaching from behind while you're on the road?

answers.text: Continue driving at the same speed.
Move to the right side and come to a stop.
answers.explanation: Upon hearing a siren or seeing the flashing lights of an emergency vehicle approaching from behind, you must facilitate its passing by safely pulling over and stopping at the right side of the road.

answers.text: Accelerate to make room.
Slow down.

explanation: Upon hearing a siren or seeing the flashing lights of an emergency vehicle approaching from behind, you must facilitate its passing by safely pulling over and stopping at the right side of the road.

question: What should you do when approaching a road with standing water?

answers.text: Assess the water depth and cross cautiously if it seems safe.
Drive through the water slowly.
Turn back and take a different path.
answers.explanation: Flooded roads can be very dangerous. Always find an alternative route.

explanation: Flooded roads can be very dangerous. Always find an alternative route.
